description_long: This object is a large, pole-mounted solar panel array designed for urban energy generation. The array consists of twelve rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a 3x4 grid, each with a dark blue-black surface and a visible grid pattern of solar cells. The panels are framed by a metallic border and are supported by a single, sturdy cylindrical pole that is anchored into a round concrete base. The structure is elevated above ground level, allowing for optimal sunlight exposure and minimizing ground footprint. The mounting pole and base appear robust, likely made of steel and concrete, to withstand outdoor conditions and provide stability. No visible electrical cabling or inverter components are present on the exterior.
description: A large, pole-mounted solar panel array with twelve dark blue photovoltaic panels in a 3x4 grid, supported by a steel pole and concrete base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full 3x4 grid of solar panels and the central supporting pole with its concrete base.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thickness of the panel array and the offset position of the supporting pole.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, clearly displaying the grid layout and the robust mounting structure.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the panel thickness and the pole's attachment to the base.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a steel pole and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid, twelve panels arranged in a rectangular matrix
